attempts.test_code:
import pytest
from source import quicksort

def test_quicksort_preserves_duplicates():
    # Input with duplicates that will expose the bug: [3, 1, 3, 2]
    # The buggy code filters with x > pivot, so duplicates of pivot (3) get dropped
    input_arr = [3, 1, 3, 2]
    result = quicksort(input_arr)
    
    # Check that the result is sorted
    assert result == sorted(input_arr), f"Expected sorted list {sorted(input_arr)}, got {result}"
    
    # Check that length is preserved (this will fail on buggy version because duplicates are dropped)
    assert len(result) == len(input_arr), f"Length mismatch: expected {len(input_arr)} elements, got {len(result)}"
